neutral.comment: If you’re wanting to sell it, probably not. If you’re planning to integrate ads as I see in that video, then maybe to plan trips somehow? 🤷‍♂️
neutral.parent: Yeah that's something I'm considering. But ultimately I'm kind of leaning that maybe that's just a part of it. It's already something not everyone will use, but as long as the people that do use it love it I think it could do well. But perhaps there are some creative ways to make it more useful to more people.

neutral.comment: Oh? How do I go about activating the 3 hour trial? I didn't get any prompt regarding it
neutral.parent: By the way, there are some sort of unavoidable issues with ios force quitting background apps if there is enough memory pressure on the phone (using things like the camera etc.) I've put in some safeguards but it won't prevent all cases. I'm still working out a way to make it even better (and it will try to restart once you've moved a certain distance since ios allows that feature), but yeah, it could be a deal breaker for some people. There are other apps vaguely similar to this and I've tested them and they all have this same issue. The only foolproof thing I could think of is some sort of push notification when then app is offloaded, but that could be annoying in its own way As for the pro features there is a 3 hour trial at the beginning. Right now the pro features are just no ads and statistics. Planning on building those out though also. Leaderboards will also be pro

positive.comment: Thank you for your thorough response! I agree there is the cost of travel aspect to consider. I’ll have to carefully think about how to handle that. As for the other comments on AI/vibe coding, yeah I didn’t vibe code this, I used some LLM tools for certain aspects, but I think using LLMs as tools is here to stay. I have a SW background though so in the case I used it it was done with care
It gets down to what I would consider the large neighborhood level at least tuned to my US point of view. I'm considering adding a level deeper, it seems to be a popular request.
positive.parent: Does it get down to street level detail for cities? I’d love to play with that

positive.comment: Hey thanks for the feedback! And I don't think I collect email, unless there's a way through App Store Connect to get user email info but I'd think that would not exist for privacy purposes
positive.parent: I downloaded and fired it up. From my initial 30 second experience: - I like the design a lot, very cool. It feels fresh and not derivative. If you are vibe coding the UI, great job. - When I see my initial location, I would probably want to engage more if there were some sort of score or sort of resource or location I could claim. Maybe think a bit about Pokemon Go mechanics to make this more exciting to users. However, that would put you in the business of a multiuser backend - The UI transition from macro to micro gets a little jarring when the bigger region fades out entirely and you are just looking at the micro. Maybe work on that a little bit for ideal UX You can email me directly from your user info database if you want to chat more.

positive.comment: No, but I would definitely consider implementing that if there were interest, seems like a good idea
Maybe you could link it to social media and unlock places by having friends there, could be cool
positive.parent: Yeah, I basically have 2 "levels" of tile resolution... the big ones I sized so that someone who dedicated a lot of effort to visiting everywhere could feasibly complete it. The smaller level is more for like "I visited all of the tiles in my home major tile", or showing where you've been like you say. But anyway, that's good feedback, thanks! Maybe I could have challenges/milestones like visiting all the major tiles in the US for example, and then a set of milestones that actually is achievable for the completionists

positive.comment: Cool idea and project. My two scents: not a big fan of the “digital” and it’s too complex. Either way, congrats and thank you for sharing. It gave me an idea for a project I had in mind.
